steps.title: Identify the key evidence
steps.content: The colon reveals the crucial fact: most patents are nonnovel, vastly outnumbering truly novel ones. This explains why simple counting would misrepresent innovation.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Belies means “contradicts” or “gives a false impression of”—exactly what a count of mostly nonnovel patents would do to one's understanding of true innovation. Portends (foretells), thwarts (prevents), and maligns (criticizes) don't fit.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
